text.title: recruitment-funnel-optimization Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for recruitment-funnel-optimization as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Optimizing conversion at one stage without checking impact on downstream quality.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Broadening screening criteria raises screen-to-interview rate but may lower interview-to-offer rate and quality-of-hire; the funnel looks better while the outcome worsens.

failure-mode.description: Using industry-wide benchmarks for specialized or executive roles.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A senior ML engineer role has structurally different conversion rates than a sales SDR role; applying the same benchmarks produces false alarms and missed signals.

failure-mode.description: Fixing symptoms (e.g., adding more job boards) without diagnosing the root stage.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If drop-off is at offer acceptance, increasing top-of-funnel volume only magnifies the waste; the fix must match the broken stage.

failure-mode.description: No candidate-experience survey, relying only on internal funnel data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Candidates who drop off after screening often don't give visible signals; exit surveys surface real reasons (slow response, poor communication) that internal data misses.
